data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/29/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb as Big Tech Earnings Loom
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher early Thursday as traders reacted to Big Tech earnings and the Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.36%, 0.25%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 3:47 a.m. EST on January 29.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major averages closed mixed, with the S&P 500 briefly crossing the 7,000 mark before ending the day down 0.01%, following the Fed's interest rate decision. Meanwhile, the DJIA rose 0.02%, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.17%, boosted by strong performances from Nvidia (NVDA) and Micron Technology (MU).

After the market closed, tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) stock dropped 6.1% due to slower cloud growth in the fiscal second quarter and softer margin guidance for the current quarter. In contrast, Meta Platforms (META) shares popped 7% on issuing a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for the first quarter and increased capex. Tesla (TSLA) stock rose 2.2% after beating Q4FY25 sales and earnings expectations despite reporting its first annual sales decline.

On the economic front, traders will watch data on weekly jobless claims, GDP growth, factory orders, and balance of trade.

Turning to earnings season, major companies reporting today include Apple (AAPL), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), and Comcast (CMCSA).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.26%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $64.17 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,532 per ounce on Thursday, surpassing the $5,500 mark for the first time amid a flight to safety as the U.S. dollar weakened.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today as traders digested corporate earnings reports.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 29, after the U.S. Fed's decision to hold rates steady and gold's continued rally.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.51%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.16%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.37%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei added 0.03%, and the Topix rose 0.28%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Is Wall Street Cautious on SoFi Technologies Stock Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Fintech SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on January 30. SOFI stock has risen 52% over the past year, driven by the company's impressive growth and continued expansion of its member base. However, shares have pulled back by more than 9% over the past month, as the company's recent capital raise has triggered concerns about shareholder dilution. Moreover, several analysts are worried about SOFI stock's high valuation.

data.title: SK Hynix Stock Gains Attention as AI Triggers 'Craziest Time Ever' Memory Shortage
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (HK:7709) warned that the global market for memory chips is tightening fast and may stay that way through 2027. Both companies said supply is being pulled toward AI data centers, leaving fewer chips for phones and PCs.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During its earnings call, SK Hynix said PC and mobile makers are already struggling to get enough supply. "PC and mobile clients are struggling to secure memory supplies," said Joo Seok Min, executive vice president of DRAM marketing at SK Hynix.

At the same time, the core issue is not weak output but limited space to expand. Samsung said cleanroom capacity will remain tight in 2026 and 2027, which limits how much new supply can be added.

Consumers and Device Makers Feel the Pressure

The shift is happening because AI memory used in servers earns much higher returns than standard memory. As a result, chip makers are sending more supply to data centers and less to consumer devices.

According to IDC, data centers may use about 70% of all memory chips made in 2026. TrendForce analyst Avril Wu called the situation "the craziest time ever," while IDC described it as a lasting shift toward AI demand.

Because of this, prices are rising across the supply chain. SK Hynix said some customers are cutting shipment plans or lowering memory specs to manage costs.

The impact is already visible in sales forecasts. Research firms now expect global smartphone shipments to fall at least 2% this year. Meanwhile, the PC market could shrink as much as 8.9% in 2026, after growing 8.1% last year.

Samsung's own mobile unit reported a 10% drop in quarterly profit. Samsung executive Cho Seong jin said 2026 will likely be a hard year, with flat or lower phone shipments due to memory prices.

How Major Companies Are Responding

Apple Inc. (AAPL) is under close watch as costs rise. Supply chain analyst Ming Chi Kuo said Apple plans to absorb higher RAM costs instead of raising prices for the iPhone 18. Apple is also moving to quarterly pricing talks with suppliers, instead of the usual six-month cycle.

Other firms are taking a different path. Lenovo Group Ltd. (LNVGY) said it expects to pass higher memory costs on to buyers. Some PC vendors have warned customers about price increases of 15% to 20%.

Synopsys Inc. (SNPS) CEO Sassine Ghazi said the shortage could last for years. "Most of the memory from the top players is going directly to AI infrastructure," he said, adding that other markets are now starved of supply.

Overall, the message from the industry is clear. As AI demand grows, memory supply is being redirected, and consumer devices are facing higher costs and tighter access that may last longer than many expected.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: EasyJet Stock Falls on Bigger-than-Expected Loss despite January Seeing the 'Strongest' Booking Period Ever
data.description:
EasyJet shares (GB:EZJ) dropped in London today after the airline reported a bigger-than-expected loss for the end of 2025. The low-cost carrier lost £93 million ($128 million) before tax in the first quarter. This is a jump from the £61 million loss they had during the same time a year ago. Even though the numbers were down, the company is very optimistic about the upcoming summer season.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A Record January Points to a Busy Summer

Even though the winter loss was higher than the £83.6 million analysts expected, the CEO is not worried. People are booking summer trips earlier than usual. Kenton Jarvis, the CEO of EasyJet, told reporters that the current demand is the best they have ever seen. "This is the strongest one we've had, not just in terms of revenue but also in terms of volume," Jarvis said.

The airline is seeing a lot of growth in its holiday business. EasyJet Holidays is currently 47% sold for the second half of the year, and prices are trending higher than last year. The company thinks that as its new routes in Italy become more popular, the initial losses will turn into long-term profits.

Why EasyJet Said No to Starlink

While many other airlines are signing deals with Elon Musk's Starlink for fast Wi-Fi, EasyJet is holding off. Jarvis mentioned that "the economics of the deal don't work" for a low-cost carrier like theirs.

Installing the large Starlink antennas on top of planes creates extra weight and wind drag. This makes the planes use more fuel, which is a major expense for budget airlines. Jarvis says that "the current offering wouldn't be good for our consumer," so they will wait for a better or cheaper technology before adding broadband to their flights.

Overall, EasyJet is spending money now to make much more money later. They had a rough winter because they spent millions opening new bases and hiring staff, but their gamble seems to be working. With more people booking holidays than ever before, the airline is positioned for a record-breaking summer. They are even being smart enough to skip expensive Wi-Fi deals to keep their ticket prices low and their fuel costs down.

Is EasyJet a Good Buy Now?

On TipRanks, EasyJet has a Hold consensus rating based on six Buys, three Holds, and three Sell ratings. The average EZJ price target of £6.06 implies 28.6% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, EZJ shares have fallen 3%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Palantir's Q4 Earnings Loom after a Stunning 100%+ Rally: What to Watch
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is set to report its fourth-quarter 2025 results on February 2, with expectations running high. The stock is up about 100% in 2025, far outpacing the broader market, driven by rising demand for its AI software.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

After such a strong run, investors are split on what comes next. Bulls point to fast growth across Palantir's Commercial and Government businesses, fueled by wider use of its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP). However, bears warn that the stock's valuation has stretched too far, leaving little room for disappointment if growth slows or market conditions turn tougher.

Here's a clear look at what bulls and bears are watching ahead of earnings.

What's Driving the Bullish Case Into Earnings?

1. Revenue Growth and AI Demand Are Accelerating: Palantir's rally has been backed by strong operating results heading into earnings. In the most recent quarter, revenue rose 63% year over year, led by a 121% jump in U.S. commercial sales and a 52% increase in U.S. government revenue. Bulls see the faster growth on the commercial side as a key sign that Palantir's AI platform is gaining traction beyond its traditional public-sector base.

2. Deal Momentum Supports the Earnings Outlook: Bulls point to Palantir's recent contract wins as proof its AI software is gaining wider use. In January, the company disclosed a multi-year enterprise agreement with the UK Ministry of Defence, covering data and AI services through 2029. Such long-term government deals provide stable revenue and remain a key driver for the stock, even as commercial growth continues to accelerate.

3. Top Analyst Stays Bullish: Some Wall Street analysts remain positive on Palantir, citing its strong position in data and AI software. Mariana Perez Mora of Bank of America recently kept a Buy rating on the stock. The analyst said Palantir's revenue growth shows no signs of slowing, fueled by strong demand from enterprise customers and expanding government contracts.

Why Some See Limited Upside

1. Rich Valuation Raises Risk: Palantir trades at very high valuation levels compared with most software peers. The stock's price-to-earnings ratio is about 231, far above the sector median of roughly 25, showing investors are paying a steep premium. Bears argue that this high bar means even strong earnings may not be enough to drive further upside if growth slows.

2. Market Sentiment Remains Cautious: Some analysts remain cautious heading into earnings, pointing to downside risk if valuation concerns persist. Recently, RBC Capital analyst Rishi Jaluria reiterated a Sell rating on PLTR stock with a $50 price target, implying roughly 70% downside. The analyst remains cautious on how durable the momentum in Palantir's commercial business will be and also believes the stock's risk-reward is "skewed to the downside."

Key Numbers to Watch in Q4 Earnings 

Wall Street expects PLTR to report earnings per share (EPS) of $0.23 for Q4 2025, reflecting a 64.3% year-over-year growth. Furthermore, revenue is expected to increase by 62% to $1.34 billion.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Waymo Swerves Past Dickens and Zebras to Get Set for London Self-Driving Launch
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL)-owned self-driving firm Waymo is coming to the streets of London this Spring after learning how to cope with the capital's Dickensian alleyways and zebra crossings.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pilot Service in April

The company said a pilot robotaxi service would be launched in April, but it hopes to operate a full fleet as soon as September. That's if, as promised by the U.K. government, regulations are changed to allow driverless taxis to operate in the city by then.

Waymo is currently mapping the streets of the capital, but the vehicles are being operated by a safety driver and are not available to hire.

That mapping includes getting used to London's busy, narrow streets and quirks. "We have been learning the nuances, such as the zebra crossings," said Ben Loewenstein, head of EU and UK policy and government affairs at Waymo.

Zebra crossings are black-and-white road markings in the U.K. where pedestrians have right of way to cross. Cars must slow down to allow passengers to do so, but it is a case of eye contact between driver and nervous crosser rather than the use of lights to ensure it ends safely. One of the most famous zebra crossings is on Abbey Road in London and was used for the cover of the Beatles album of the same name in 1969.

Rivals Ready to Launch

Loewenstein was speaking at an event at the London Transport Museum in Covent Garden today where Waymo unveiled one of the Jaguar cars it hopes to use in the capital.

Waymo's cars are electric Jaguar iPACE vehicles while rivals Lyft (LYFT) and Uber (UBER) -who are also keen to launch services once the regulations change – have both chosen to partner with Chinese operator Baidu (BIDU).

In London, Waymo will work with Moove, a fleet management firm that handles maintenance and charging for ride-hailing companies. The partnership will help manage Waymo's electric cars and local operations. Waymo's system uses radar and lidar sensors to guide vehicles on the road, and the company says it has logged over 100 million miles without human control.

Waymo's move marks its first expansion into Europe and its second international market after Tokyo. The company already runs commercial robotaxi services in U.S. cities including San Francisco, Los Angeles, and Austin.
data.author: David Craik
